join_order.notes: is_big[] dense char array size 60000001 (~57MB); O(1) membership test per orders row
Dense array index 5.7MB fully L3-resident; only ~2,520 lookups total — negligible cost

filters.predicate: SUM(l_quantity) GROUP BY l_orderkey HAVING SUM > 300
filters.notes: HAVING filter after atomic accumulation; ~2,520 qualifying orderkeys
Evaluated via is_big[o_orderkey] direct char array lookup; ~2,520 rows survive from 15M

data_structures.pass1_qty_accumulator: shared_atomic_array(std::atomic<int16_t>, size=60000001, ~120MB, mmap MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_PRIVATE zero-init); all 64 threads do relaxed fetch_add directly; no per-thread copies, no merge phase; int16_t sufficient since max sum per orderkey = 7 lineitems x 50 qty = 350 < 32767
data_structures.qualifying_filter: dense_array(char, size=60000001, ~57MB); built with single parallel sequential scan of shared accumulator after Pass 1: is_big[k] = (qty_by_order[k].load(relaxed) > 300)

pass1_reuse_optimization.description: The subquery SUM(l_quantity) per l_orderkey and the outer query SUM(l_quantity) per group are identical. The shared atomic accumulator built in Pass 1 already contains correct final sum_qty values. No second lineitem scan is needed.

parallelism.pass1_lineitem_scan: all nthreads share one std::atomic<int16_t>[60000001] array; each thread processes its morsel stripe; uses memory_order_relaxed fetch_add; no merge step after scan completes
parallelism.build_is_big: parallel scan of shared accumulator: each thread fills its key-space slice of is_big[] with (qty > 300) check; also builds a small vector of qualifying orderkeys for result assembly
parallelism.pass2_orders_scan: parallel morsel scan of orders columns (o_orderkey, o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice); each thread collects local Q18Result vector for qualifying rows; merge vectors after scan

indexes.notes: Resolves o_custkey -> customer row in O(1); 5.7MB fully L3-resident; invoked ~2,520 times

fields.name: o_orderdate
fields.source: orders/o_orderdate.bin (days_since_epoch; decode to YYYY-MM-DD for output only)

fields.name: sum_qty
fields.source: qty_by_order[o_orderkey].load(relaxed) from Pass 1 shared atomic accumulator, cast to int32_t

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category B — algorithm change): iter_0 allocates 64 thread-local int32_t accumulators of 229 MB each (14.7 GB total), then merges them with a parallel element-wise reduction that reads 63 × 229 MB = 14.4 GB of memory traffic — the dominant cost in the hot case at ~73ms on a 200 GB/s system. The fix replaces all thread-local arrays with a single shared std::atomic<int16_t>[60000001] array (120 MB total, mmap MAP_ANONYMOUS for zero-init). All 64 threads write directly to this shared array using memory_order_relaxed fetch_add, eliminating the merge phase entirely. int16_t is correct because the maximum sum per orderkey is 7 lineitems × 50 max_qty = 350, well within int16_t range (32767). The halved element size (2 vs 4 bytes) also improves cache efficiency: 120 MB / 64 bytes = 1.875M cache lines vs 3.75M for int32_t, with 32 entries per cache line giving better temporal locality for orders with nearby orderkeys. Atomic contention is negligible: P(two threads hitting the same key) ≈ 64²/15M ≈ 0.03%. All other phases (Pass 2 orders scan, customer join, sort) are unchanged from iter_0.
